data.body:
PASS 1 / T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification): PASS — no findings.

The bright-line fires (novel architectural pattern + a migration/seeding step), and the plan clears T1 on all three checks:

(a) Prior art is CONSIDERED, not ignored. The plan explicitly names the in-tree precedent — the `local_label_intent` "ever-seen" set projected from raw event history (verified at src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/local_label_intent.py and its consumption in outbound_differ._diff_labels) — and diagnoses its concrete failure mode: `compact_ticket` collapses the event log to a SNAPSHOT whose compiled_state holds only CURRENT refs, so the event-history projection fails closed. External field prior art (Unito/Exalate/FolderSync/Syncthing/ghas-jira-integration) is cited as validating the intent-gate concept.

(b) The novelty is JUSTIFIED vs the established approach (anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three). Rather than re-using the label-intent event-scan pattern, the plan moves provenance into a reducer-maintained `compiled_state.managed_refs` set precisely because that survives compaction "for free" via process_snapshot — verified at src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:515-519, where process_snapshot blindly restores every compiled_state key. The Rule-of-Three is satisfied: the primitive is built once and consumed by parent (tan-elbow-mica) + links (wake-inn-parse) + documented future peers, not a one-off repackage.

(c) Capability assertions are resolved against the code: the load-bearing "compiled_state restored across compaction" claim is confirmed by process_snapshot's behavior; the migration is a lazy in-memory reseed on first absence (no DDL), consistent with the event-sourced model.

Affirmations: relevant prior art considered before committing; novel pattern's novelty justified vs the established (event-history-scan) approach; load-bearing capability assertion (compaction-survival via process_snapshot) verified.
